---
title: "Obedience to Authority"
categories: [social, conformity]
---

Obedience to authority in a financial context means following expert advice even when the data or our views say otherwise. We therefore trust the expert's status as a substitute for our own, independent evaluation.

::: {.callout-note icon=false collapse="false"}
## Example

#### Expert endorsement
An financial commentator suggests investing in crypto. However, their recommendation might (a) also be biased, whether they realise it (e.g. sponsored by a betting platform) or not (simply carried away by the ongoing trend) (b) be mistaken, either by lack of true expertise or simply because crypto markets are hard to predict.

!["Sounds like a real expert, this guy".](../images/obedience-to-authority.jpg){width="450px" fig-align="center"}

::: {.also-relates}
**Also relates to:** [Social Contagion](social-contagion.qmd) · [Communal Reinforcement](communal-reinforcement.qmd) · [Availability Cascades](availability-cascades.qmd) · [Confirmation Bias](confirmation-bias.qmd)
:::

:::
